specific_mechanism: Vernalization in Arabidopsis: prolonged cold exposure (~6-8 weeks) drives Polycomb Repressive Complex 2 (PRC2) to deposit H3K27me3 at the FLOWERING LOCUS C (FLC) gene, silencing flowering-repression. Each cell switches stochastically into the silenced state with probability rising with cold duration. The population-level fraction of FLC-silenced cells determines whether the plant can flower. Once silenced, the state is mitotically heritable through subsequent warm seasons. A generational reset re-opens FLC in the next embryo.
llm_application: Long-time-window integration commitment mechanism for agent runtime: a population of per-component switches (per-prompt-template, per-routing-rule, per-skill-threshold) each flip stochastically with probability rising with cumulative exposure to a tenant-specific contextual signal. Once a population-level threshold is crossed, the agent commits to a behavioral mode that persists across sessions, distinct from gradient-fine-tuning (global instant) and in-context-learning (instant local). Explicit reset events (new tenant, new policy) dissolve the commitment population-wide.
novelty_claim: Persistent agent memory and preference accumulation are studied (Spring AI AutoMemoryTools, Cloudflare Agent Memory, A-Mem) and stochastic agent behavior is documented (EcoGym 2602.09514, Shutdownable Agents 2604.17502). Epigenetic memory ↔ neural network analogy is established (PMC12418969 neuro-symbolic epigenetic regulation; Science adg3053 3D epigenetic memory). The vernalization-specific FLC-PRC2-H3K27me3 framing as a slow-integration-with-population-threshold mechanism for LLM agent commitment has not been imported as far as I can find.
